snippets.title: One-sample t-test with SciPy
snippets.code:
from scipy import stats

sample = [112, 118, 125, 130, 115, 122, 119, 128, 121, 117]
mu0 = 130.0

t_stat, p_value = stats.ttest_1samp(sample, popmean=mu0)
print(f"t = {t_stat:.3f}, two-sided p = {p_value:.4f}")

alpha = 0.05
if p_value <= alpha:
    print("Reject H0 at alpha = 0.05")
else:
    print("Fail to reject H0 at alpha = 0.05")
snippets.explanation: `ttest_1samp` computes t = (x̄ − μ₀)/(s/√n) and a two-sided p-value from the t-distribution with n−1 degrees of freedom.

snippets.title: Two-sample t-test for independent groups
snippets.code:
from scipy import stats

baseline = [0.71, 0.68, 0.74, 0.70, 0.72, 0.69, 0.73, 0.70]
new_prompt = [0.75, 0.77, 0.74, 0.76, 0.78, 0.73, 0.75, 0.77]

t_stat, p_value = stats.ttest_ind(baseline, new_prompt, equal_var=False)
print(f"Welch t = {t_stat:.3f}, p = {p_value:.4f}")
snippets.explanation: Welch's t-test (equal_var=False) compares two independent means without assuming equal variances—common in messy ML experiment logs.

snippets.title: Manual t statistic and critical value
snippets.code:
import numpy as np
from scipy import stats

x = np.array([118, 122, 115, 130, 119, 125, 121, 117, 124, 120])
mu0 = 130.0
n = len(x)
xbar = x.mean()
s = x.std(ddof=1)

t_manual = (xbar - mu0) / (s / np.sqrt(n))
df = n - 1
p_two_sided = 2 * stats.t.sf(abs(t_manual), df)

print(f"xbar={xbar:.2f}, t={t_manual:.3f}, p={p_two_sided:.4f}")
snippets.explanation: Building t by hand clarifies the intuition: observed distance from μ₀ divided by standard error, then referenced to the t-distribution.

snippets.title: Confidence interval dual to two-sided test
snippets.code:
import numpy as np
from scipy import stats

x = np.array([118, 122, 115, 130, 119, 125, 121, 117, 124, 120])
mu0 = 130.0
n = len(x)
mean = x.mean()
se = x.std(ddof=1) / np.sqrt(n)
ci = stats.t.interval(0.95, df=n - 1, loc=mean, scale=se)

print(f"95% CI for mean: ({ci[0]:.2f}, {ci[1]:.2f})")
print("Reject H0: mu=130?", mu0 < ci[0] or mu0 > ci[1])
snippets.explanation: If μ₀ falls outside the 95% CI, the two-sided α=0.05 test rejects H₀: μ=μ₀—the interval shows where plausible means lie.
